result: I’d make every human mind born with an un-erasable, felt sense that every other mind is as entire and real as their own. Not a rule to obey, but an internal axiom—like the felt certainty that fire burns—so that cruelty requires the same deliberate self-mutilation as sticking your own hand in the flame. The externals of injustice wouldn’t vanish overnight, but every policy, invention, or private act would have to run the gauntlet of that visceral knowledge. We’d still disagree, compete, even fight, but we’d do it knowing we were severing parts of ourselves in the process.

prompt: If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
